metadata.title: First Brands collapse + UBS/O'Connor: the off-balance-sheet private-credit canary
metadata.provenance: Web-verified (Bloomberg, AdvisorHub, finews, ABF Journal, swissinfo, Fed FSR reporting).
metadata.why_it_matters: First Brands is the 2025 dress rehearsal for the SAME off-balance-sheet financing structure used to fund AI datacenters (Stargate SPV, Meta-Hyperion/Blue Owl JV, GPU-collateralized neocloud debt). It shows how opaque factoring/supply-chain-finance + private credit can hide leverage until a sudden, fraudulent-or-not unwind - and how the losses surface at banks (UBS, Jefferies) and nonbanks simultaneously.

events.event: First Brands Group (auto-parts roll-up) files Chapter 11.
Fraud allegations detailed: ~$700M misappropriated, invoices inflated ~50x, $2.3B in missing receivables; alleged fraudulent operation 2018-2025; financed by Jefferies, UBS and multiple banks.
UBS winds down two O'Connor Working Capital Finance Opportunistic funds with First Brands exposure.

US Federal Reserve Financial Stability Report explicitly cites an 'auto parts supplier' bankruptcy and warns on 'risks from opaque off-balance-sheet funding, potentially amplifying spillovers to banks and nonbanks.'

metrics.name: UBS HFS + O'Connor total First Brands exposure
metrics.notes: Largest unsecured creditor group: $349.8M supply-chain-finance claims (HFS $233.7M + O'Connor $116.1M unsecured) plus secured exposure pushing total >$500M.

metrics.name: O'Connor Working Capital fund concentration in First Brands
metrics.notes: Single-name concentration far beyond standard portfolio limits - the fragility signature of reaching for yield in opaque private credit.

metrics.name: Expected UBS fund NAV recovery
metrics.notes: ~$0.70 on the dollar expected to be monetized by year-end - i.e. ~30% loss on a supposedly low-risk invoice-finance strategy.

metrics.name: Missing receivables
metrics.notes: $2.3B of receivables financed multiple times / unaccounted - the off-balance-sheet leakage.

chain_edges.notes: Loss path: opaque factoring obligor -> private-credit fund -> bank-affiliated asset manager.
Jefferies/Leucadia (Point Bonita) also exposed; second canary alongside Tricolor (subprime auto) in 2025.

linkage_to_ai_bubble: Same structural DNA as AI-datacenter financing: leverage moved off the balance sheet into factoring/SPVs/private credit, invisible until a discrete unwind, with losses landing at BOTH a globally-systemic bank (UBS) and nonbanks. The Fed naming it in the FSR is the regulator pre-positioning for the bigger version of this risk sitting in Stargate/Hyperion/GPU-backed neocloud debt (see macro-cre-privatecredit.json).
